because.text: Zero-relay implementation cycle per the PR #4 pattern: brief (evidence) -> codex exec (gpt-5.5) in isolated clone -> maintainer cold verification -> this warrant. Delivered: Python reference (+406) and Go parity (+888) for SPEC s5.1/s7/s9 - tunnel + outcome fingerprints + settle verb + relitigation refusal, settlement-active root derivation with policy-authorized adoption, advisory genesis.json (pinned-hash or unused + WARN), threshold policy parsing with ERR on invalid, key-state derivation in DAG order with maximal-conflict detection and conflicted-key exclusion. tests/settlement.py: 13 differential scenarios, identical warning strings enforced across impls. Cold verification: full acceptance block green; plain mode BYTE-IDENTICAL to v0.2 behavior (old-vs-new diff empty; sigma-glyph 18-record store agrees across impls); validate_body untouched - zero body schema changes. Maintainer spot-check found the one brief case the run skipped - stale-rotation-replay non-conflict, the exact attack DeepSeek refuted at the GOV-001 gate - added it, both impls pass. Implementation convention accepted: key-rotation subject blobs are verifier-local JCS {actor,key} (SPEC mandates semantics, not an interchange format). Wired into agree_check.sh and CI. GOV-001 arc complete: Kimi's governance critique -> 3-family gate -> SPEC v0.3 -> differential implementation, tagged v0.3.

subject.note: SPEC v0.3 implementation gate: settlement/multi-root/key-state in BOTH impls (codex zero-relay, 1732 lines), settlement differential ALL AGREE incl. maintainer spot-check
